message:
fix(connection): concurrent snapshot visibility floor, JOIN column collation resolution for SELECT DISTINCT

Concurrent snapshot visibility floor:
- When building a snapshot from a pager publication, now takes the max
  of the publication's commit sequence and the connection's local
  memdb_visible_commit_seq, ensuring the snapshot never regresses below
  what the connection has already seen locally
- Enhanced debug tracing logs all three values (publication, local,
  chosen) for diagnosing stale-read issues

Collation resolution for JOIN columns in SELECT DISTINCT:
- select_result_collations previously used agg_arg_collation for all
  expression columns, which could not resolve the correct collation
  when a column came from a joined table via a qualified alias (e.g.,
  r.name)
- Introduces FromClauseCollationLookup that pre-indexes all source
  columns from the FROM clause with their collations, handling
  NATURAL/USING join deduplication
- select_expr_effective_collation now consults this lookup for column
  references, correctly resolving collation from the actual source
  table rather than falling back
- Four new tests: qualified alias collation resolution, USING join
  collation (left side wins per SQLite semantics), end-to-end DISTINCT
  with qualified join alias vs rusqlite oracle, end-to-end DISTINCT
  with USING join vs oracle

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
